case_name: Schilling v. Quincy Physicians & Surgeons Clinic, S.C.

summary.brief: Schilling v. Quincy Physicians & Surgeons Clinic, S.C.. Medical malpractice case involving surgery orthopaedic.

evidence_index.text: Plaintiff went to a podiatrist on January 30, 2017, and described his ongoing symptoms. The podiatrist ordered X-rays of the foot.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Those X-rays showed a complete dislocated fracture of the first metatarsal and minimally displaced complete transverse fractures of the base of the second and third metatarsals.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Plaintiff had surgery on his foot in March 2017. Plaintiff later developed an infection and had a second surgery in October 2017.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Plaintiff ultimately had his left leg amputated just below the knee.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Plaintiff’s complaint alleged that Dr. Love had misdiagnosed a fracture in plaintiff’s foot as an infection.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Following a jury trial in the circuit court of Adams County, a verdict was returned in favor of defendants.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The Appellate Court, Fourth District, affirmed the trial court.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The appellate court concluded that the trial court did not abuse its discretion in denying plaintiff’s motions for a mistrial.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: The appellate court also rejected plaintiff’s claim that the Prim instruction was coercive.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: The trial court’s denial of plaintiff’s motion for a mistrial was not arbitrary or fanciful.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: We affirm the judgment of the appellate court, which affirmed the judgment of the circuit court.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Difficulty walking on left foot with pain
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient reported pain began a week prior after injuring left ankle and foot. Complained of increased redness and swelling into ankles and lower extremities with increased pain in and along the bottom of the foot.

focused_exam.foot: Painful to walk, unable to put pressure on the upper part of the foot

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Left foot edema and potential cellulitis

requestables.name: X-ray of the foot
requestables.description: X-ray to assess for fractures or dislocations

reveal.result_summary: Complete dislocated fracture of the first metatarsal and minimally displaced complete transverse fractures of the base of the second and third metatarsals
reveal.clinical_significance: Indicates significant fracture requiring orthopedic intervention
reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt referral to orthopedic surgeon

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with foot pain and difficulty walking

key_events.event: Follow-up visit and continued diagnosis of cellulitis
Referral to podiatrist and X-ray ordered

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with ongoing foot pain and swelling, initially diagnosed with cellulitis
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management?

options.clinical_reasoning: Necessary to rule out fracture given the patient's symptoms and history of injury
Assumed infection without ruling out fracture

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant continued with antibiotics for cellulitis
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant diagnosed cellulitis and prescribed antibiotics

expected_action_court.description: X-ray should have been ordered to rule out fracture
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence indicated that imaging was necessary given the symptoms

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to order imaging led to misdiagnosis and inappropriate treatment
explanation.what_should_have_happened: X-ray should have been ordered to confirm or rule out fracture
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed an infection and had a second surgery, ultimately leading to amputation below the knee
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Loss of limb, requiring prosthetic and rehabilitation

key_findings: Jury found in favor of defendants
Appellate court affirmed the trial court's decision

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to order appropriate imaging initially
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Early imaging could have prevented further injury and amputation

factual_timeline.event: Patient examined by Dr. Love at QMC

tests_performed.result: Complete dislocated fracture of the first metatarsal and minimally displaced complete transverse fractures of the base of the second and third metatarsals

court_findings.legal_significance: Jury verdict in favor of defendants affirmed by appellate court
